verdict: 2026-07-13 08:42:09 PDT — NOT merge-ready. The Terms rewrite, opportunity-cost note, and roadmap archive are genuinely resolved, and privacy.html now accurately qualifies the unchanged /api/log sink. However, the reconciled live documentation remains internally inconsistent. No dependency manifest changed, and the deletion-only server shape remains sound.

findings.title: Public security page still promises the removed encrypted store
findings.claim: The live, navigation-linked Security overview still says presenter configuration is AES-256-GCM encrypted under a per-user Zoom-account key and retained until deletion (lines 54–70). That directly contradicts the session-only implementation, Privacy Policy, Terms, and AC6.
findings.alternative: Rewrite the data-handling and retention sections around the current invariant: presenter configuration is browser-session-only and never persisted server-side; retain accurate TLS, headers, OAuth-processing, and operational-logging disclosures.
findings.win: Eliminates the most visible remaining false public security and retention contract and makes all marketplace-facing pages describe the same product.

findings.title: The architecture SVG fix left its central model and legend unchanged
findings.claim: Although the SVG description and SDK bullet were updated, the visible side-panel box still says the presenter sets each person's rate, and the legend still says names and opportunity-cost values are encrypted and stored on the server at line 101. The architecture diagram therefore continues to present both removed models as current.
findings.alternative: Update the visible side-panel copy and legend—not only the metadata—to show one manual attendee count × one average rate, held in the browser session, with no individual configuration flow to the server.
findings.win: Removes the two remaining contradictions from the primary architecture artifact and makes the diagram usable as a dependable review document.

findings.title: Logging guarantees remain stronger than the unchanged sink outside privacy.html
findings.claim: privacy.html was correctly narrowed, but the README still calls the logs “PII-free” and guarantees they carry no names or rates; the data-retention and security policies likewise say diagnostics exclude participant PII and contain “no arbitrary payloads.” In contrast, server/src/app.js:107–115 accepts and serializes any JSON body up to 100 KB. These live claims conflict with both the sink and the newly qualified Privacy Policy.
findings.alternative: Apply privacy.html’s qualified wording consistently: describe the intended client-generated fields and source minimization, disclose that the sink records the submitted body, and avoid categorical PII-free/no-arbitrary-payload guarantees unless server-side schema allowlisting is added in a separately scoped code change.
findings.win: Centralizes one truthful logging posture across public and internal policy documents without expanding this teardown into new server behavior.
